records.ety: [OE. isterdai, AS. geostran dæg, from geostran, geostra, giestran, gistran, gystran, yesterday (akin to D. gisteren, G. gestern, OHG. gestaron, Icel. gær yesterday, to-morrow, Goth. gistradagis to-morrow, L. heri yesterday, Gr. <?/, Skr. hyas) + dæg day. Cf. Hestern. <?/<?/<?/<?/.]

defs: The day last past; the day next before the present.
quotes: All our yesterdays have lighted fools The way to dusty death.
We are but of yesterday, and know nothing.

defs: Fig.: A recent time; time not long past.
quotes: The proudest royal houses are but of yesterday, when compared with the line of supreme pontiffs.

defs: On the day last past; on the day preceding to-day; as, the affair took place yesterday.
